title: Line Detection Strategies
text: Line detection 3D point clouds essential identifying: Infrastructure: Power lines, cables, transmission lines Building edges: Roof ridges, wall corners, structural beams Urban features: Street edges, fence lines, railings Industrial scans: Pipes, conduits, framework elements detect_lines() function uses RANSAC-based 3D line fitting critical feature: context-aware detection via in_scene parameter.

title: Line Detection Strategies
text: Use : point cloud contains primarily linear features (cables, wires, infrastructure edges) Behavior: Aggressive detection relaxed parameters - Distance threshold increased 30% - Min votes reduced 70% - Min length reduced 80% - Max iterations increased 50% Example use cases: - Power line corridor scans - Cable/wire detection - Edge-extractions

title: Line Detection Strategies
text: Use : Lines coexist shapes (planes, cylinders, buildings) Behavior: Stricter detection using exact parameters - detects standalone lines - Avoids detecting edges planes separate lines - Use detecting shapes Example use cases: - Urban scenes buildings standalone cables - Forestry terrain fallen logs - Industrial scans structures conduits

title: Line Detection Strategies
code: library(houghPrimitives)  # Detect lines result <- detect_lines(   xyz = point_matrix,       # N x 3 matrix   distance_threshold = 0.05, # Max distance from line   min_votes = 30,           # Min points per line   min_length = 1.0,         # Min line length (meters)   max_iterations = 500,     # RANSAC iterations   in_scene = TRUE,          # Context mode   label_points = FALSE      # Return labels? )  cat("Detected", result$n_lines, "lines\n") print(result$lines)

text: Maximum perpendicular distance line point considered part line. Impact: - small: Miss valid line points, fragmented lines - large: Include noise, merge nearby parallel lines

title: Line Detection Strategies
code: set.seed(42)  # Generate random 3D line generate_line <- function(x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2, n_points = 100, noise = 0.02) {   t <- runif(n_points, 0, 1)   x <- x1 + t * (x2 - x1) + rnorm(n_points, sd = noise)   y <- y1 + t * (y2 - y1) + rnorm(n_points, sd = noise)   z <- z1 + t * (z2 - z1) + rnorm(n_points, sd = noise)   cbind(x, y, z) }  # Create 3 lines line1 <- generate_line(0, 0, 0, 10, 5, 3, n_points = 120) line2 <- generate_line(5, 10, 1, 15, 15, 5, n_points = 100) line3 <- generate_line(2, 2, 4, 8, 12, 6, n_points = 150)  # Add noise noise <- cbind(runif(100, 0, 20), runif(100, 0, 20), runif(100, 0, 6))  # Combine all_points <- rbind(line1, line2, line3, noise)  # Detect with aggressive mode (line-only) result <- detect_lines(   all_points,   distance_threshold = 0.05,   min_votes = 30,   min_length = 2.0,   in_scene = FALSE,  # Line-only mode   label_points = TRUE )  cat("Detected", result$n_lines, "/ 3 lines\n") print(result$lines)

title: Line Detection Strategies
text: Post-process results filter direction:
code: result <- detect_lines(xyz, label_points = TRUE)  # Calculate line directions lines <- result$lines dx <- lines$x2 - lines$x1 dy <- lines$y2 - lines$y1 dz <- lines$z2 - lines$z1 len <- sqrt(dx^2 + dy^2 + dz^2)  # Unit direction vectors ux <- dx / len uy <- dy / len uz <- dz / len  # Filter nearly horizontal lines (|uz| < 0.2) horizontal_mask <- abs(uz) < 0.2 horizontal_lines <- lines[horizontal_mask, ]  cat("Found", nrow(horizontal_lines), "horizontal lines\n")  # Filter nearly vertical lines (|uz| > 0.9) vertical_mask <- abs(uz) > 0.9 vertical_lines <- lines[vertical_mask, ]  cat("Found", nrow(vertical_lines), "vertical lines\n")

title: Line Detection Strategies
text: detect_lines() function provides flexible 3D line detection : ✓ Context-aware modes via in_scene parameter ✓ RANSAC-based robust fitting ✓ Configurable sensitivity parameters ✓ Point labeling analysis Key Decision: - Line-scenes: Use in_scene = FALSE aggressive detection - Complex scenes: Use in_scene = TRUE detecting shapes Critical workflow tip: detecting multiple shape types, always detect lines planes avoid false positives planar edges.

title: Multi-Shape Scene Classification
text: Real-world point clouds often contain multiple shape types coexisting scene: Urban environments: Buildings (planes/cuboids), trees (cylinders), street furniture (spheres), power lines (lines) Industrial facilities: Tanks (cylinders/spheres), pipes (cylinders/lines), structural beams (lines), walls (planes) Forestry: Trees (cylinders), terrain (planes), rocks (spheres), fallen logs (cylinders/lines) vignette demonstrates classify shapes complex scene using houghPrimitives package.

title: Multi-Shape Scene Classification
text: Critical insight: order detect shapes significantly impacts results : detection “consumes” points cloud Dominant features (planes) can mask smaller features (lines) shapes subsets others (cube edges vs standalone lines)

title: Multi-Shape Scene Classification
text: order? - Detect distinctive/rare shapes first (spheres, cylinders) - Detect thin features dominant ones (lines planes) - Detect composite shapes last (cubes/cuboids depend plane detection)
code: # OPTIMAL ORDER for most complex scenes:  # 1. Spheres - Compact, geometrically distinct spheres <- detect_spheres(xyz, ...)  # 2. Cylinders - Elongated, less common than planes cylinders <- detect_cylinders(xyz, ...)  # 3. Lines - Thin features (BEFORE planes!) lines <- detect_lines(xyz, in_scene = TRUE, ...)  # 4. Planes - Large, dominant features planes <- detect_planes(xyz, ...)  # 5. Cubes/Cuboids - Composite structures (last) cubes <- detect_cubes(xyz, ...) cuboids <- detect_cuboids(xyz, ...)

title: Multi-Shape Scene Classification
code:
# Create unified classification n_points <- nrow(xyz) point_shape_type <- rep(0, n_points)  # 0 = unclassified point_shape_id <- rep(0, n_points)    # 0 = no shape  # Shape type codes SPHERE <- 1 CYLINDER <- 2 LINE <- 3 PLANE <- 4 CUBE <- 5  # Assign labels (last assignment wins for overlaps) if (shapes$spheres$n_spheres > 0) {   mask <- shapes$spheres$point_labels > 0   point_shape_type[mask] <- SPHERE   point_shape_id[mask] <- shapes$spheres$point_labels[mask] }  if (shapes$cylinders$n_cylinders > 0) {   mask <- shapes$cylinders$point_labels > 0   point_shape_type[mask] <- CYLINDER   point_shape_id[mask] <- shapes$cylinders$point_labels[mask] }  if (shapes$lines$n_lines > 0) {   mask <- shapes$lines$point_labels > 0   point_shape_type[mask] <- LINE   point_shape_id[mask] <- shapes$lines$point_labels[mask] }  if (shapes$planes$n_planes > 0) {   mask <- shapes$planes$point_labels > 0   point_shape_type[mask] <- PLANE   point_shape_id[mask] <- shapes$planes$point_labels[mask] }  if (shapes$cubes$n_cubes > 0) {   mask <- shapes$cubes$point_labels > 0   point_shape_type[mask] <- CUBE   point_shape_id[mask] <- shapes$cubes$point_labels[mask] }  # Summary statistics cat("
=== Classification Summary ===
") cat(sprintf("Total points: %d
", n_points)) cat(sprintf("Spheres: %d points (%.1f%%)
",              sum(point_shape_type == SPHERE),             100 * sum(point_shape_type == SPHERE) / n_points)) cat(sprintf("Cylinders: %d points (%.1f%%)
",              sum(point_shape_type == CYLINDER),             100 * sum(point_shape_type == CYLINDER) / n_points)) cat(sprintf("Lines: %d points (%.1f%%)
",              sum(point_shape_type == LINE),             100 * sum(point_shape_type == LINE) / n_points)) cat(sprintf("Planes: %d points (%.1f%%)
",              sum(point_shape_type == PLANE),             100 * sum(point_shape_type == PLANE) / n_points)) cat(sprintf("Cubes: %d points (%.1f%%)
",              sum(point_shape_type == CUBE),             100 * sum(point_shape_type == CUBE) / n_points)) cat(sprintf("Unclassified: %d points (%.1f%%)
",              sum(point_shape_type == 0),             100 * sum(point_shape_type == 0) / n_points))

title: Multi-Shape Scene Classification
code:
library(houghPrimitives) set.seed(123)  # ============================================================================ # GENERATE SYNTHETIC SCENE # ============================================================================  # Helper function: Generate sphere generate_sphere <- function(cx, cy, cz, radius, n_points = 200) {   theta <- runif(n_points, 0, 2 * pi)   phi <- acos(2 * runif(n_points) - 1)   x <- cx + radius * sin(phi) * cos(theta)   y <- cy + radius * sin(phi) * sin(theta)   z <- cz + radius * cos(phi)   cbind(x, y, z) }  # Helper function: Generate cylinder generate_cylinder <- function(cx, cy, z1, z2, radius, n_points = 250) {   theta <- runif(n_points, 0, 2 * pi)   z <- runif(n_points, z1, z2)   x <- cx + radius * cos(theta)   y <- cy + radius * sin(theta)   cbind(x, y, z) }  # Helper function: Generate plane generate_plane <- function(z_level, xrange, yrange, n_points = 400) {   x <- runif(n_points, xrange[1], xrange[2])   y <- runif(n_points, yrange[1], yrange[2])   z <- rep(z_level, n_points) + rnorm(n_points, sd = 0.02)   cbind(x, y, z) }  # Helper function: Generate line generate_line <- function(x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2, n_points = 100, noise = 0.02) {   t <- runif(n_points, 0, 1)   x <- x1 + t * (x2 - x1) + rnorm(n_points, sd = noise)   y <- y1 + t * (y2 - y1) + rnorm(n_points, sd = noise)   z <- z1 + t * (z2 - z1) + rnorm(n_points, sd = noise)   cbind(x, y, z) }  # Create scene components cat("Generating synthetic scene...
")  # Ground plane ground <- generate_plane(0, c(0, 20), c(0, 20), n_points = 500)  # 2 spheres sphere1 <- generate_sphere(5, 5, 2, radius = 0.8, n_points = 200) sphere2 <- generate_sphere(15, 15, 1.5, radius = 1.0, n_points = 250)  # 3 cylinders (poles) cyl1 <- generate_cylinder(10, 5, 0, 5, radius = 0.3, n_points = 300) cyl2 <- generate_cylinder(5, 15, 0, 4.5, radius = 0.35, n_points = 280) cyl3 <- generate_cylinder(15, 8, 0, 6, radius = 0.28, n_points = 320)  # 2 lines (cables) line1 <- generate_line(0, 8, 5, 20, 10, 5.5, n_points = 120, noise = 0.015) line2 <- generate_line(3, 0, 4.5, 17, 20, 4.8, n_points = 140, noise = 0.015)  # Wall (vertical plane) wall <- cbind(   rep(18, 300),   runif(300, 5, 15),   runif(300, 0, 5) )  # Uniform noise noise <- cbind(runif(200, 0, 20), runif(200, 0, 20), runif(200, 0, 6))  # Combine all scene <- rbind(   ground, sphere1, sphere2,   cyl1, cyl2, cyl3,   line1, line2, wall, noise )  cat(sprintf("Scene created: %d points
", nrow(scene))) cat(sprintf("  Ground: %d
", nrow(ground))) cat(sprintf("  Spheres: %d (2 objects)
", nrow(sphere1) + nrow(sphere2))) cat(sprintf("  Cylinders: %d (3 objects)
", nrow(cyl1) + nrow(cyl2) + nrow(cyl3))) cat(sprintf("  Lines: %d (2 objects)
", nrow(line1) + nrow(line2))) cat(sprintf("  Wall: %d
", nrow(wall))) cat(sprintf("  Noise: %d
", nrow(noise)))  # ============================================================================ # CLASSIFY ALL SHAPES # ============================================================================  cat("
=== SHAPE DETECTION ===
")  # 1. Spheres spheres <- detect_spheres(scene,                           min_radius = 0.6, max_radius = 1.2,                          min_votes = 80, label_points = TRUE) cat(sprintf("Spheres: %d detected
", spheres$n_spheres))  # 2. Cylinders cylinders <- detect_cylinders(scene,                               min_radius = 0.25, max_radius = 0.4,                               min_length = 3.0, min_votes = 100,                               label_points = TRUE) cat(sprintf("Cylinders: %d detected
", cylinders$n_cylinders))  # 3. Lines (strict mode) lines <- detect_lines(scene,                       distance_threshold = 0.04, min_votes = 50,                       min_length = 3.0, in_scene = TRUE,                       label_points = TRUE) cat(sprintf("Lines: %d detected
", lines$n_lines))  # 4. Planes planes <- detect_planes(scene,                         distance_threshold = 0.08, min_votes = 120,                         max_iterations = 800, label_points = TRUE) cat(sprintf("Planes: %d detected
", planes$n_planes))  # ============================================================================ # COMBINE CLASSIFICATIONS # ============================================================================  # Create unified labels classification <- rep("Unclassified", nrow(scene)) classification[spheres$point_labels > 0] <- "Sphere" classification[cylinders$point_labels > 0] <- "Cylinder" classification[lines$point_labels > 0] <- "Line" classification[planes$point_labels > 0] <- "Plane"  # Shape codes for lidR shape_code <- rep(0, nrow(scene)) shape_code[classification == "Sphere"] <- 1 shape_code[classification == "Cylinder"] <- 2 shape_code[classification == "Line"] <- 3 shape_code[classification == "Plane"] <- 4  # Summary cat("
=== CLASSIFICATION RESULTS ===
") table(classification) cat(sprintf("
Classification rate: %.1f%%
",             100 * sum(shape_code > 0) / nrow(scene)))  # ============================================================================ # VISUALIZE # ============================================================================  library(lidR)  las <- LAS(data.frame(   X = scene[, 1],   Y = scene[, 2],   Z = scene[, 3],   ShapeType = shape_code,   Classification = factor(classification) ))  # Plot by shape type plot(las, color = "Classification", size = 3,      main = "Multi-Shape Scene Classification")

title: Multi-Shape Scene Classification
text: ’s complete example synthetic data 3D visualization:  Multi-shape classification showing detected spheres (coral), cylinder (blue), plane (green).
code: library(rgl)  # Generate synthetic multi-shape scene set.seed(456)  # Helper functions generate_sphere <- function(cx, cy, cz, radius, n_points = 150) {   theta <- runif(n_points, 0, 2 * pi)   phi <- acos(2 * runif(n_points) - 1)   x <- cx + radius * sin(phi) * cos(theta)   y <- cy + radius * sin(phi) * sin(theta)   z <- cz + radius * cos(phi)   cbind(x, y, z) }  generate_cylinder <- function(cx, cy, z1, z2, radius, n_points = 200) {   theta <- runif(n_points, 0, 2 * pi)   z <- runif(n_points, z1, z2)   x <- cx + radius * cos(theta)   y <- cy + radius * sin(theta)   cbind(x, y, z) }  generate_plane <- function(z_level, xrange, yrange, n_points = 300) {   x <- runif(n_points, xrange[1], xrange[2])   y <- runif(n_points, yrange[1], yrange[2])   z <- rep(z_level, n_points) + rnorm(n_points, sd = 0.02)   cbind(x, y, z) }  # Create scene components ground <- generate_plane(0, c(0, 10), c(0, 10), n_points = 400) sphere1 <- generate_sphere(2, 2, 1, 0.8, n_points = 200) sphere2 <- generate_sphere(7, 3, 0.6, 0.6, n_points = 150) pole <- generate_cylinder(5, 7, 0, 4, 0.3, n_points = 250)  # Combine all_points <- rbind(ground, sphere1, sphere2, pole)  # Detect shapes in optimal order spheres <- detect_spheres(all_points, min_radius = 0.4, max_radius = 1.0,                            min_votes = 50, label_points = TRUE) cylinders <- detect_cylinders(all_points, min_radius = 0.2, max_radius = 0.4,                               min_length = 2.0, min_votes = 80, label_points = TRUE) planes <- detect_planes(all_points, distance_threshold = 0.05,                         min_votes = 150, label_points = TRUE)  # Create classification classification <- rep(0, nrow(all_points)) classification[spheres$point_labels > 0] <- 1 classification[cylinders$point_labels > 0] <- 2 classification[planes$point_labels > 0] <- 3  # Visualize open3d() #> null  #>    8 shape_colors <- c("gray70", "coral", "steelblue", "forestgreen") shape_sizes <- c(3, 6, 6, 4)  for (i in 0:3) {   mask <- classification == i   if (any(mask)) {     points3d(all_points[mask, 1], all_points[mask, 2], all_points[mask, 3],              color = shape_colors[i + 1], size = shape_sizes[i + 1])   } }  axes3d() title3d(sprintf("Multi-Shape: %d Spheres, %d Cylinders, %d Planes",                 spheres$n_spheres, cylinders$n_cylinders, planes$n_planes),         "X", "Y", "Z") # title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Successful shape detection point clouds requires proper parameter tuning. guide provides systematic approaches optimize detection parameters specific data.

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: shape detection involves balancing: Precision: percentage detected shapes correct? (minimize false positives) Recall: percentage true shapes detected? (minimize false negatives)
code: High min_votes → High precision, Low recall (fewer false positives, miss some shapes) Low min_votes  → Low precision, High recall (detect more shapes, more false positives)

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Parameters depend : Point density: Points per square meter/cubic meter Noise level: Measurement accuracy, scanner quality Scale: Size objects (cm vs meters vs km) Completeness: Partial vs complete shape coverage

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: parameters appear multiple detection functions:

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Meaning: Minimum number points required form valid shape set: Quick rules thumb:
code: # STEP 1: Estimate point density bbox <- apply(xyz, 2, range) volume <- prod(bbox[2,] - bbox[1,]) density <- nrow(xyz) / volume cat(sprintf("Point density: %.1f points/m³\n", density))  # STEP 2: Estimate expected points on shape # For sphere with radius r: surface_area = 4 * pi * r^2 expected_sphere_points <- density * 4 * pi * (1.0)^2  # 1m radius cat(sprintf("Expected points on 1m sphere: %.0f\n", expected_sphere_points))  # STEP 3: Set min_votes to 30-50% of expected min_votes <- round(expected_sphere_points * 0.4) # Dense scans (>100 pts/m³) min_votes_sphere <- 80 min_votes_cylinder <- 100 min_votes_plane <- 200  # Medium density (20-100 pts/m³) min_votes_sphere <- 50 min_votes_cylinder <- 60 min_votes_plane <- 150  # Sparse scans (<20 pts/m³) min_votes_sphere <- 30 min_votes_cylinder <- 40 min_votes_plane <- 100

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Meaning: Maximum perpendicular distance ideal shape point inclusion set: Adaptive approach:
code: # Based on scanner accuracy and noise # Typical values:  # High-accuracy scanner (mm precision) distance_threshold <- 0.02  # 2cm  # Standard terrestrial scanner (cm precision) distance_threshold <- 0.05  # 5cm  # Airborne/UAV LiDAR distance_threshold <- 0.10  # 10cm  # Mobile mapping / kinematic distance_threshold <- 0.15  # 15cm # Calculate noise level from ground plane ground_points <- xyz[xyz[,3] < quantile(xyz[,3], 0.1), ]  # Bottom 10% ground_fit <- lm(Z ~ X + Y, data = as.data.frame(ground_points)) residuals <- abs(residuals(ground_fit)) noise_level <- quantile(residuals, 0.95)  # 95th percentile  # Set threshold to 2-3x noise level distance_threshold <- 3 * noise_level cat(sprintf("Estimated noise: %.3fm, threshold: %.3fm\n",              noise_level, distance_threshold))

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Meaning: Number RANSAC iterations (planes, lines) set:
code: # Based on expected inlier ratio # More noise → need more iterations  # Formula: iterations = log(1 - confidence) / log(1 - inlier_ratio^samples)  calculate_iterations <- function(confidence = 0.99, inlier_ratio = 0.5,                                    n_samples = 3) {   iter <- log(1 - confidence) / log(1 - inlier_ratio^n_samples)   ceiling(iter) }  # High inlier ratio (clean data) calculate_iterations(inlier_ratio = 0.7)  # ~20 iterations  # Medium inlier ratio (typical) calculate_iterations(inlier_ratio = 0.5)  # ~35 iterations  # Low inlier ratio (noisy scene) calculate_iterations(inlier_ratio = 0.3)  # ~200 iterations  # Practical defaults: # Clean data: max_iterations = 300 # Normal data: max_iterations = 500-800 # Noisy data: max_iterations = 1000-2000

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Parameter selection: Tuning strategy:
code: detect_spheres(xyz,                min_radius = ?,      # Smallest sphere to detect                max_radius = ?,      # Largest sphere to detect                min_votes = ?)       # Minimum points per sphere # 1. RADIUS RANGE: Based on expected object sizes  # Small objects (balls, markers) min_radius <- 0.05  # 5cm max_radius <- 0.20  # 20cm  # Medium objects (decorative spheres) min_radius <- 0.30 max_radius <- 2.0  # Large structures (tank ends, domes) min_radius <- 2.0 max_radius <- 10.0  # 2. MIN_VOTES: Based on point density and radius  # For dense scans: ~100 points per m² of sphere surface points_per_m2 <- 100 sphere_surface <- 4 * pi * (0.5)^2  # 0.5m radius min_votes <- points_per_m2 * sphere_surface  # ~314 points  # Practical: 30-50% of theoretical min_votes <- round(min_votes * 0.4)  # ~125 points # Start conservative result1 <- detect_spheres(xyz, min_radius = 0.5, max_radius = 1.5, min_votes = 100)  # Too few detected? Relax constraints result2 <- detect_spheres(xyz, min_radius = 0.3, max_radius = 2.0, min_votes = 60)  # Too many false positives? Tighten result3 <- detect_spheres(xyz, min_radius = 0.6, max_radius = 1.2, min_votes = 120)

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Parameter selection: Special case: Tree boles
code: detect_cylinders(xyz,                  min_radius = ?,     # Minimum cylinder radius                  max_radius = ?,     # Maximum cylinder radius                  min_length = ?,     # Minimum cylinder length                  min_votes = ?)      # Minimum points # RADIUS: Based on expected cylinders  # Thin poles/trees min_radius <- 0.05  # 5cm (small tree) max_radius <- 0.30  # 30cm (large tree)  # Standard poles (street lights) min_radius <- 0.10 max_radius <- 0.40  # Large cylinders (tanks, silos) min_radius <- 1.0 max_radius <- 5.0  # LENGTH: Minimum visible extent  # Forest (partial trunk visible) min_length <- 1.0  # At least 1m of trunk  # Urban poles (full height usually visible) min_length <- 2.5  # At least 2.5m tall  # MIN_VOTES: Larger than spheres (more surface area)  # Dense scan, medium cylinder (r=0.3m, h=5m) surface_area <- 2 * pi * 0.3 * 5  # ~9.4 m² min_votes <- 100 * 9.4 * 0.3  # points/m² × area × factor ≈ 280 detect_tree_boles(xyz,                   min_radius = 0.05,      # Smallest tree DBH                   max_radius = 0.5,       # Largest expected                   min_height = 1.0,       # Minimum visible trunk                   vertical_only = TRUE,   # Only vertical cylinders                   min_votes = 50)         # Lower due to gaps/occlusion

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Parameter selection: Context-dependent tuning:
code: detect_lines(xyz,              distance_threshold = ?,  # Max distance from line              min_votes = ?,          # Min points per line              min_length = ?,         # Min line length              in_scene = ?)           # Context mode # DISTANCE_THRESHOLD: Based on line thickness  # Thin features (cables) distance_threshold <- 0.02  # 2cm  # Edges (building corners) distance_threshold <- 0.05  # 5cm  # Thick beams distance_threshold <- 0.10  # 10cm  # MIN_LENGTH: Avoid spurious short segments  # Cables (want long segments) min_length <- 3.0  # At least 3m  # Any linear feature min_length <- 1.0  # MIN_VOTES: Typically fewer than other shapes  # Thin cables (fewer points) min_votes <- 30  # Standard edges min_votes <- 50  # IN_SCENE: CRITICAL parameter!  # Line-only data (power lines scan) in_scene <- FALSE  # Aggressive mode  # Complex scenes (with buildings, terrain) in_scene <- TRUE   # Strict mode (detect AFTER other shapes) # Scenario 1: Power line corridor (lines only) lines <- detect_lines(xyz,                       distance_threshold = 0.04,                       min_votes = 25,                       min_length = 5.0,                       in_scene = FALSE,  # Aggressive                       max_iterations = 1200)  # Scenario 2: Urban scene (lines + buildings) # First detect other shapes planes <- detect_planes(xyz, ...) cylinders <- detect_cylinders(xyz, ...)  # Then detect standalone lines lines <- detect_lines(xyz,                       distance_threshold = 0.06,                       min_votes = 40,                       min_length = 2.0,                       in_scene = TRUE,   # Strict                       max_iterations = 800)

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
text: Parameter selection:
code: detect_planes(xyz,               distance_threshold = ?,  # Max point-to-plane distance               min_votes = ?,          # Min points per plane               max_iterations = ?)     # RANSAC iterations # DISTANCE_THRESHOLD: Related to surface roughness  # Smooth walls, floors (indoor) distance_threshold <- 0.03  # 3cm  # Building facades (outdoor) distance_threshold <- 0.10  # 10cm  # Terrain (ground plane with vegetation) distance_threshold <- 0.20  # 20cm  # MIN_VOTES: Largest of all shapes (planes are big!)  # Indoor rooms min_votes <- 200  # Building walls min_votes <- 300  # Terrain/ground min_votes <- 500  # MAX_ITERATIONS: Medium to high  # Clean building scans max_iterations <- 500  # Complex scenes max_iterations <- 800  # Very noisy data max_iterations <- 1500

code: library(houghPrimitives)  # Get basic statistics summary <- summarize_points(xyz) print(summary)  # Calculate density volume <- prod(summary$extent) density <- summary$n_points / volume cat(sprintf("Point density: %.1f pts/m³\n", density))  # Estimate noise from ground ground_subset <- xyz[xyz[,3] < quantile(xyz[,3], 0.05), ] if (nrow(ground_subset) > 100) {   fit <- lm(V3 ~ V1 + V2, data = as.data.frame(ground_subset))   noise <- sd(residuals(fit))   cat(sprintf("Estimated noise: %.3fm\n", noise)) }  # Visualize distribution hist(xyz[,3], main = "Z Distribution", xlab = "Height (m)")

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
code: # Use conservative defaults first defaults <- list(   spheres = list(     min_radius = 0.5, max_radius = 2.0, min_votes = 50   ),   cylinders = list(     min_radius = 0.2, max_radius = 0.5,      min_length = 2.0, min_votes = 80   ),   lines = list(     distance_threshold = 0.05, min_votes = 40,     min_length = 2.0, in_scene = TRUE   ),   planes = list(     distance_threshold = 0.10, min_votes = 150,     max_iterations = 800   ) )  # Test each detector spheres <- detect_spheres(xyz,                           min_radius = defaults$spheres$min_radius,                          max_radius = defaults$spheres$max_radius,                          min_votes = defaults$spheres$min_votes) cat("Spheres:", spheres$n_spheres, "\n")

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
code: # Create tuning grid tune_sphere_detection <- function(xyz, radius_range, vote_range) {   results <- data.frame()      for (min_r in radius_range) {     for (min_v in vote_range) {       result <- detect_spheres(xyz,                               min_radius = min_r,                               max_radius = min_r * 2,                               min_votes = min_v)              results <- rbind(results, data.frame(         min_radius = min_r,         min_votes = min_v,         n_detected = result$n_spheres       ))     }   }      results }  # Run grid search grid_results <- tune_sphere_detection(   xyz,   radius_range = c(0.3, 0.5, 0.7, 1.0),   vote_range = c(30, 50, 80, 100) )  print(grid_results)  # Visualize library(ggplot2) ggplot(grid_results, aes(x = min_radius, y = min_votes, fill = n_detected)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "blue") +   labs(title = "Sphere Detection Grid Search")

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
code: # If you have ground truth (e.g., manual labeling) true_spheres <- 5  # Known number  # Test different parameters test_params <- function(min_votes) {   result <- detect_spheres(xyz, min_votes = min_votes)      # Metrics   detected <- result$n_spheres   precision <- min(detected, true_spheres) / max(detected, 1)   recall <- min(detected, true_spheres) / true_spheres   f1 <- 2 * precision * recall / (precision + recall)      data.frame(min_votes = min_votes, detected = detected,              precision = precision, recall = recall, f1 = f1) }  # Test range param_tests <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq(20, 100, by = 10), test_params)) print(param_tests)  # Find optimal (max F1 score) optimal_idx <- which.max(param_tests$f1) cat("Optimal min_votes:", param_tests$min_votes[optimal_idx], "\n")

title: Parameter Tuning Guide
code: # Automatically adapt parameters to data characteristics  adaptive_sphere_params <- function(xyz) {   # Estimate density   volume <- prod(apply(xyz, 2, function(x) diff(range(x))))   density <- nrow(xyz) / volume      # Adapt min_votes   if (density > 100) {     min_votes <- 80   } else if (density > 50) {     min_votes <- 50   } else {     min_votes <- 30   }      # Estimate scale from point cloud extent   extent <- apply(xyz, 2, function(x) diff(range(x)))   avg_extent <- mean(extent)      # Adapt radius range   if (avg_extent < 10) {  # Small scene     min_radius <- 0.05     max_radius <- 1.0   } else if (avg_extent < 100) {  # Medium scene     min_radius <- 0.3     max_radius <- 3.0   } else {  # Large scene     min_radius <- 1.0     max_radius <- 10.0   }      list(min_votes = min_votes, min_radius = min_radius, max_radius = max_radius) }  # Use adaptive parameters params <- adaptive_sphere_params(xyz) result <- detect_spheres(xyz,                          min_votes = params$min_votes,                         min_radius = params$min_radius,                         max_radius = params$max_radius)

title: Sphere Detection in Point Clouds
text: Sphere detection useful identifying various spherical objects point clouds: Decorative balls urban scenes Tank ends dome structures Spherical markers targets Tree crowns (approximate) Industrial spherical components detect_spheres() function uses spatial clustering combined geometric validation identify spheres 3D point clouds.

title: Sphere Detection in Point Clouds
text: algorithm follows steps: Spatial Clustering: Groups nearby points using 3D grid-based clustering Center Estimation: Iteratively refines sphere center using geometric relationships Radius Calculation: Computes radius mean distance center surface points Validation: Checks circularity, octant coverage, radius consistency Filtering: Removes candidates don’t meet geometric criteria

title: Sphere Detection in Point Clouds
text: Unlike simple centroid calculation, algorithm uses geometric relationship: center=point−radius×direction\text{center} = \text{point} - \text{radius} \times \text{direction} iterative approach (3 iterations) correctly identifies sphere center even though surface points don’t average center.

title: Sphere Detection in Point Clouds
code:
library(houghPrimitives) set.seed(42)  # Function to generate sphere surface points generate_sphere <- function(cx, cy, cz, radius, n_points = 200) {   # Uniform distribution on sphere surface   theta <- runif(n_points, 0, 2 * pi)   phi <- acos(2 * runif(n_points) - 1)      x <- cx + radius * sin(phi) * cos(theta)   y <- cy + radius * sin(phi) * sin(theta)   z <- cz + radius * cos(phi)      cbind(x, y, z) }  # Generate 3 spheres sphere1 <- generate_sphere(5, 5, 3, radius = 1.2, n_points = 300) sphere2 <- generate_sphere(12, 8, 2.5, radius = 0.8, n_points = 250) sphere3 <- generate_sphere(8, 15, 4, radius = 1.5, n_points = 400)  # Add uniform noise noise <- cbind(   runif(300, 0, 20),   runif(300, 0, 20),   runif(300, 0, 6) )  # Combine all_points <- rbind(sphere1, sphere2, sphere3, noise)  # Detect spheres result <- detect_spheres(   all_points,   min_radius = 0.5,   max_radius = 2.0,   min_votes = 50,   label_points = TRUE )  cat("Detected", result$n_spheres, "spheres
") print(result$spheres)  # Calculate accuracy n_sphere_points <- nrow(sphere1) + nrow(sphere2) + nrow(sphere3) labels <- result$point_labels  true_positives <- sum(labels[1:n_sphere_points] > 0) precision <- true_positives / sum(labels > 0) recall <- true_positives / n_sphere_points  cat(sprintf("Precision: %.2f%%
", precision * 100)) cat(sprintf("Recall: %.2f%%
", recall * 100))

Detect spheres result <- detect_spheres(all_points, min_radius = 0.5, max_radius = 2.0,                         min_votes = 50, label_points = TRUE)  # Create 3D visualization open3d() #> null  #>    8  # Overlay detected sphere (semi-transparent)     sphere_params <- result$spheres[i, ]     spheres3d(sphere_params$x,                sphere_params$y,                sphere_params$z,               radius = sphere_params$radius,               color = colors[i], alpha = 0.2)   } }  axes3d() title3d(sprintf("Detected %d Spheres", result$n_spheres),          "X (m)", "Y (m)", "Z (m)") # # Assume first N points are sphere points, rest is noise n_true_spheres <- 950 labels <- result$point_labels  # True positives: sphere points correctly classified tp <- sum(labels[1:n_true_spheres] > 0)  # False positives: noise classified as spheres fp <- sum(labels[(n_true_spheres + 1):length(labels)] > 0)  # False negatives: sphere points missed fn <- sum(labels[1:n_true_spheres] == 0)  precision <- tp / (tp + fp) recall <- tp / (tp + fn) f1_score <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)  cat(sprintf("Precision: %.2f%%

title: Line Detection in Different Scenarios
text: Use : Detecting lines (cables, edges, infrastructure scans) Behavior: - aggressive detection - Distance threshold increased 30% - Min votes reduced 70% specified value - Min length reduced 80% specified value - Max iterations increased 50% Example:
code: # Detecting cables in infrastructure scan result <- detect_lines(cable_points,                         distance_threshold = 0.05,                        min_votes = 30,                        min_length = 2.0,                        in_scene = FALSE)  # Find ALL lines

title: Line Detection in Different Scenarios
text: Use : Lines exist shapes (buildings, terrain, cylinders) Behavior: - Stricter detection - Uses specified parameters -- finds standalone lines part structures - Detects lines planes/cylinders identified Example:
code: # Complex scene - detect other shapes first planes <- detect_planes(scene, label_points = TRUE) cylinders <- detect_cylinders(scene, label_points = TRUE)  # Then detect standalone lines from remaining points lines <- detect_lines(scene,                       distance_threshold = 0.06,                       min_votes = 40,                       in_scene = TRUE)  # Strict, standalone only

title: Line Detection in Different Scenarios
text: scenes multiple shape types: line-scenarios:
code: # 1. Detect planes first (they're usually dominant) planes_result <- detect_planes(points, label_points = TRUE)  # 2. Detect spheres/cylinders spheres_result <- detect_spheres(points, label_points = TRUE) cylinders_result <- detect_cylinders(points, label_points = TRUE)  # 3. Detect standalone lines (from remaining points) lines_result <- detect_lines(points,                                in_scene = TRUE,  # Strict mode                               label_points = TRUE)  # 4. Detect cubes/cuboids last cubes_result <- detect_cubes(points, label_points = TRUE) # Just detect all lines aggressively lines_result <- detect_lines(cable_scan,                               distance_threshold = 0.05,                               min_votes = 30,                               in_scene = FALSE,  # Aggressive mode                               label_points = TRUE)

title: Line Detection in Different Scenarios
text: in_scene parameter adjusts detection parameters internally C++ code: ensures optimal detection use case without requiring users manually tune many parameters.
code: // When in_scene = FALSE (line-only mode) if (!in_scene) {   distance_threshold *= 1.3;  // More tolerance   min_votes = max(10, (int)(min_votes * 0.7));  // Lower requirement   min_length *= 0.8;  // Accept shorter   max_iterations = (int)(max_iterations * 1.5);  // Search harder }

title: NAMESPACE Fix for Rcpp Registration
text: Previously, running roxygen2::roxygenize() regenerate NAMESPACE file omit critical directives needed Rcpp C++ function registration: - useDynLib(houghPrimitives, .registration = TRUE) - importFrom(Rcpp,sourceCpp) caused detect_* functions fail errors like:
code: Error: .Call function '_houghPrimitives_detect_spheres' not available

title: NAMESPACE Fix for Rcpp Registration
text: Created R/houghPrimitives-package.R roxygen2 directives:
code: #' @keywords internal #' @aliases houghPrimitives-package "_PACKAGE"  ## usethis namespace: start #' @useDynLib houghPrimitives, .registration = TRUE #' @importFrom Rcpp sourceCpp ## usethis namespace: end NULL

title: NAMESPACE Fix for Rcpp Registration
text: @useDynLib roxygen tag tells roxygen2 include useDynLib() directive NAMESPACE @importFrom roxygen tag tells roxygen2 include importFrom() directive directives now automatically maintained roxygen2 package builds special "_PACKAGE" string creates package-level documentation

title: NAMESPACE Fix for Rcpp Registration
text: fix persists across multiple roxygen2::roxygenize() calls. NAMESPACE now always includes:
code: importFrom(Rcpp,sourceCpp) useDynLib(houghPrimitives, .registration = TRUE)

title: houghPrimitives Color Palettes
text: Primary palette function - Returns n colors specified palette. Parameters: - n - Number colors return - type - Palette type: - "full" - 20-color palette dark bright (sequential) - "contrast" - 19-color high-contrast palette (categorical) Behavior: - n <= palette_size: Returns first n colors - n > palette_size: Cycles palette using rep_len() Examples:
code: # Get 5 colors from full palette colors <- hough_palette(5, "full") # Returns: "#0B0E14", "#1F2937", "#2E3440", "#FF9F1C", "#FF7A18"  # Get 8 high-contrast colors colors <- hough_palette(8, "contrast") # Returns: "#FF9F1C", "#4CC9F0", "#E4572E", "#2EC4B6", ...  # Request more than available (cycles) colors <- hough_palette(25, "contrast")  # 19-color palette # Returns 25 colors, cycling after 19

title: houghPrimitives Color Palettes
text: Interpolated gradient function - Creates smooth color transitions. Parameters: - n - Number interpolated colors - type - Base palette type - alpha - Transparency (0.0 = fully transparent, 1.0 = opaque) Behavior: - Uses colorRampPalette() interpolate base colors - Skips dark anchors maintain visibility - Creates smooth gradients continuous data Examples:
code: # Smooth 50-color gradient for elevation mapping elevation_colors <- hough_palette_gradient(50, "full")  # 20-color gradient with transparency for overlays overlay_colors <- hough_palette_gradient(20, "contrast", alpha = 0.7)

title: houghPrimitives Color Palettes
text: Contrasting pairs function - Returns high-contrast foreground/background pairs. Parameters: - n - Number color pairs (returns 2*n colors) Behavior: - Returns predefined high-contrast pairs - Pairs alternate: (foreground1, background1, foreground2, background2, ...) - Maximum 10 pairs available (cycles n > 10) Examples:
code: # Get 3 contrasting pairs (6 colors) pairs <- hough_palette_pairs(3) # Returns: "#FF9F1C", "#0B0E14", "#4CC9F0", "#1F2937", "#E4572E", "#FFFFFF"  # Use for text labels on shapes for (i in 1:3) {   fg <- pairs[2*i - 1]  # Foreground color   bg <- pairs[2*i]      # Background color   # Draw text with fg color on bg background }

title: houghPrimitives Color Palettes
text: Visualization function - Displays barplot palette colors. Parameters: - n - Number colors preview - type - Palette type Behavior: - Creates barplot bar colored according palette - Useful exploring color combinations - Shows color hex codes bars Examples:
code: # Preview 12 contrast colors hough_palette_preview(12, "contrast")  # Preview full palette gradient hough_palette_preview(20, "full")

title: houghPrimitives Color Palettes
code: # Assign colors based on shape ID (not detection order) # This ensures same shape gets same color across runs  assign_colors_by_id <- function(shape_ids, palette_type = "contrast") {   unique_ids <- sort(unique(shape_ids))   n_shapes <- length(unique_ids)      # Get colors for all unique IDs   palette <- hough_palette(n_shapes, palette_type)      # Create mapping: ID -> Color   color_map <- setNames(palette, unique_ids)      # Apply to all points   colors <- color_map[as.character(shape_ids)]   return(colors) }

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide
text: Typical values: - Young forest: min_radius = 0.05, max_radius = 0.3 - Mature forest: min_radius = 0.15, max_radius = 0.8 - Dense forest: increase min_votes 50-100 - Sparse forest: decrease min_votes 20-30
code: detect_tree_boles(   las,   min_radius = 0.05,      # Minimum trunk radius (m)   max_radius = 1.0,       # Maximum trunk radius (m)   min_height = 0.5,       # Minimum visible trunk height (m)   min_votes = 30,         # Minimum supporting points   vertical_only = TRUE,   # Only detect vertical trunks   label_points = TRUE     # Label all points )

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide
text: Typical values: - Urban areas: min_volume = 50, min_votes = 200 - Rural buildings: min_volume = 10, min_votes = 100 - Small structures: min_volume = 1, min_votes = 50
code: detect_buildings(   las,   min_volume = 1.0,       # Minimum building volume (m³)   min_votes = 100,        # Minimum supporting points   axis_aligned = FALSE,   # Allow rotated buildings   label_points = TRUE )

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide
text: Typical values: - Ground detection: distance_threshold = 0.1 - Wall detection: distance_threshold = 0.05 - Fine features: distance_threshold = 0.02
code: detect_planes(   las,   max_planes = 0,             # 0 = detect all   min_votes = 50,             # Minimum supporting points   distance_threshold = 0.05,  # Max distance to plane (m)   label_points = TRUE )

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide
code: # 1. Load and prepare data las <- readLAS("forest.las") las <- normalize_height(las, tin())  # 2. Filter to stem height stems <- filter_poi(las, Z >= 1.0 & Z <= 2.0)  # 3. Detect trees trees <- detect_tree_boles(stems, min_radius = 0.08, max_radius = 0.6)  # 4. Calculate metrics dbh <- trees$radii * 2 * 100  # Convert to DBH in cm n_trees <- trees$n_cylinders basal_area <- sum(pi * trees$radii^2)  # 5. Export las <- add_shape_labels(las, trees) writeLAS(las, "trees_detected.las")

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide
code: # 1. Load urban data las <- readLAS("urban.las")  # 2. Remove ground las_ng <- filter_poi(las, Classification != 2)  # 3. Detect buildings buildings <- detect_buildings(las_ng, min_volume = 25)  # 4. Calculate statistics volumes <- buildings$dimensions[,1] * buildings$dimensions[,2] * buildings$dimensions[,3] heights <- buildings$dimensions[,3]  # 5. Export footprints export_shapes(buildings, "buildings", crs = st_crs(las))

title: houghPrimitives Quick Reference Guide
code: # 1. Load data las <- readLAS("scene.las")  # 2. Classify all features result <- classify_point_shapes(las)  # 3. Add labels las <- add_shape_labels(las, result)  # 4. Extract components trees <- filter_poi(las, ShapeType == 4) buildings <- filter_poi(las, ShapeType == 5) ground <- filter_poi(las, ShapeType == 2)  # 5. Save writeLAS(las, "classified.las")

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Decorative balls/spheres urban scenes - Tank ends, dome structures - Spherical objects industrial scans Parameters: - min_radius, max_radius: Expected radius range - min_votes: Minimum points required per sphere - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels sphere IDs Returns: - n_spheres: Number detected spheres - spheres: DataFrame (x, y, z, radius, n_points) - point_labels (requested): Integer vector sphere IDs (0 = sphere)
code: detect_spheres(xyz,                min_radius = 0.1,                max_radius = 2.0,                min_votes = 30,                label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Poles, pillars - Pipe detection - General cylindrical objects (orientation) Parameters: - min_radius, max_radius: Expected radius range - min_length: Minimum cylinder length - min_votes: Minimum points per cylinder - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels Returns: - n_cylinders: Number detected - cylinders: DataFrame (cylinder_id, x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2, radius, n_points) - (x1,y1,z1) (x2,y2,z2) endpoints cylinder axis - point_labels (requested): Integer vector
code: detect_cylinders(xyz,                  min_radius = 0.1,                  max_radius = 2.0,                  min_length = 1.0,                  min_votes = 50,                  label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Forest inventory - Urban tree detection - Vertical cylinder structures Parameters: - min_radius, max_radius: Expected trunk radius - min_height: Minimum visible trunk height - min_votes: Minimum points per tree - vertical_only: TRUE, detect vertical cylinders - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels Returns: - n_trees: Number detected - trees: DataFrame (tree_id, x, y, radius, height, n_points) - point_labels (requested): Integer vector tree IDs
code: detect_tree_boles(xyz,                   min_radius = 0.05,                   max_radius = 0.5,                   min_height = 1.0,                   min_votes = 30,                   vertical_only = TRUE,                   label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Ground extraction - Wall/facade detection - Roof planes - flat surface Parameters: - distance_threshold: Max distance point plane - min_votes: Minimum points per plane - max_iterations: RANSAC iterations - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels Returns: - n_planes: Number detected - planes: DataFrame (plane_id, , b, c, d, n_points) - Plane equation: ax + + cz + d = 0 - point_labels (requested): Integer vector Algorithm: RANSAC 3-point plane fitting
code: detect_planes(xyz,               distance_threshold = 0.1,               min_votes = 100,               max_iterations = 1000,               label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Power lines, cables - Beams, edges - Linear features Parameters: - distance_threshold: Max perpendicular distance point line - min_votes: Minimum points per line - min_length: Minimum line length - max_iterations: RANSAC iterations - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels Returns: - n_lines: Number detected - lines: DataFrame (line_id, x1, y1, z1, x2, y2, z2, n_points) - (x1,y1,z1) (x2,y2,z2) line endpoints - point_labels (requested): Integer vector Algorithm: RANSAC 2-point line fitting
code: detect_lines(xyz,              distance_threshold = 0.05,              min_votes = 20,              min_length = 0.5,              max_iterations = 500,              label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Storage boxes - Cubic containers - Regular box-shaped objects Parameters: - min_size, max_size: Expected cube side length range - min_votes: Minimum total points per cube - plane_threshold: Distance threshold plane detection - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels Returns: - n_cubes: Number detected - cubes: DataFrame (cube_id, x, y, z, size, n_points) - (x,y,z) center, size edge length - point_labels (requested): Integer vector Algorithm: 1. First detects planes using RANSAC 2. Searches 6 orthogonal planes forming regular cube 3. Validates cube dimensions Note: Requires 6 faces well-represented point cloud. Success varies : - Point density face - Noise levels - Occlusion (missing faces)
code: detect_cubes(xyz,              min_size = 0.3,              max_size = 3.0,              min_votes = 100,              plane_threshold = 0.05,              label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Use Cases: - Building detection - Rectangular containers - Box-like structures (non-cubic) Parameters: - min_width, max_width: Expected width/depth range - min_height, max_height: Expected height range - min_votes: Minimum total points per cuboid - plane_threshold: Distance threshold plane detection - label_points: TRUE, returns point labels Returns: - n_cuboids: Number detected - cuboids: DataFrame (cuboid_id, x, y, z, width, depth, height, n_points) - (x,y,z) center - point_labels (requested): Integer vector Algorithm: 1. Detects planes using RANSAC 2. Searches 4+ orthogonal/parallel planes forming rectangular boxes 3. Computes bounding box dimensions Note: Similar challenges cube detection. May produce variable results depending scene complexity.
code: detect_cuboids(xyz,                min_width = 0.5,                max_width = 10.0,                min_height = 0.5,                max_height = 10.0,                min_votes = 150,                plane_threshold = 0.1,                label_points = FALSE)

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
code: library(houghPrimitives)  # Generate synthetic sphere theta <- runif(200, 0, 2*pi) phi <- acos(2*runif(200) - 1) x <- 5 + 1.0 * sin(phi) * cos(theta) y <- 5 + 1.0 * sin(phi) * sin(theta) z <- 2 + 1.0 * cos(phi) points <- cbind(x, y, z)  # Detect result <- detect_spheres(points, min_radius = 0.8, max_radius = 1.2, min_votes = 50) print(result$spheres) #   sphere_id   x   y   z radius n_points # 1         1 5.0 5.0 2.0    1.0      200

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Point Density: Higher density improves detection accuracy Noise: Lower noise levels improve detection methods Parameter Tuning: Adjust min_votes thresholds based data

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: Spheres: - Works best uniform point distribution sphere surface - Increase min_votes avoid detecting small sphere fragments Cylinders: - Ensure cylinder axis well-sampled - tree detection, use detect_tree_boles() vertical cylinders Planes: - Larger distance_threshold noisy data - Increase max_iterations complex scenes Lines: - Tight distance_threshold clean line detection - Ensure lines consistent point density Cubes/Cuboids: - Require good sampling multiple faces - May fail faces occluded poorly sampled - Consider using detect_planes() directly analyzing plane relationships - Best clean, well-structured synthetic data high-quality scans

title: Shape Detection Guide for houghPrimitives
text: detection functions can label points, making visualization easy:
code: library(lidR) library(houghPrimitives)  # Detect shapes with labeling spheres <- detect_spheres(points, label_points = TRUE) planes <- detect_planes(points, label_points = TRUE)  # Create LAS object with classifications las <- LAS(data.frame(   X = points[,1],   Y = points[,2],   Z = points[,3],   Classification = ifelse(spheres$point_labels > 0, 7L,                    ifelse(planes$point_labels > 0, 2L, 1L)) ))  # Interactive 3D plot plot(las, color = "Classification", size = 3)

title: Quick Start Guide
text: package now exports 8 functions detecting 6 primitive shape types: detect_spheres() - Detect spherical objects detect_cylinders() - Detect cylinders (orientation) detect_tree_boles() - Detect vertical cylinders (trees/poles) detect_planes() - Detect planar surfaces detect_lines() - Detect 3D lines detect_cubes() - Detect cubic boxes (via plane analysis) detect_cuboids() - Detect rectangular structures (via plane analysis) summarize_points() - Get basic point cloud statistics
